sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
The **Trello** connector integrates Shielva with the [Trello REST API v1](https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/trello/rest/) (base URL: `https://api.trello.com/1/`) to sync boards and cards from your Trello account into your Shielva knowledge base.

Trello is a Kanban-style project management tool by Atlassian, used by teams worldwide to organise work on boards, lists, and cards.

**Key capabilities:**
- Sync all open boards and their cards in a single `sync()` call
- Boards normalized with `type='board'`, cards with `type='card'` â€” both with `source='trello'`
- Stable 16-character document IDs: `sha256('board:' + board_id)[:16]` and `sha256('card:' + card_id)[:16]`
- Direct access to board lists, members, and labels via dedicated methods
- API key + token query-parameter authentication â€” no Authorization header
- Typed exception hierarchy with automatic retry (exponential backoff, 3 attempts)
- Auth errors never retried â€” fail-fast on invalid or revoked credentials

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
Get the Trello connector running in under 5 minutes.

### 1. Obtain your API Key and Token

1. Visit [https://trello.com/app-key](https://trello.com/app-key) while logged in.
2. Copy your **API Key** (32 characters) from the top of the page.
3. Click the **Token** link, click **Allow**, and copy the 64-character token.

### 2. Install

In the Shielva integration builder:
1. Navigate to **Integrations â†’ Trello**.
2. Paste your **API Key** and **Token** into the respective fields.
3. Click **Install**.

The connector calls `GET /members/me` to validate credentials and returns the connected user's username on success.

### 3. Run a sync

```python
async with TrelloConnector(config={"api_key": "YOUR_KEY", "token": "YOUR_TOKEN"}) as conn:
    result = await conn.sync(kb_id="kb_trello_001")
    print(f"Synced {result.documents_synced} documents")
```

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
The Trello connector uses **query-parameter authentication** â€” credentials are appended to every URL, not sent in an Authorization header.

### Credential fields

| Field | Key | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|-----|------|----------|-------------|
| API Key | `api_key` | string | Yes | 32-character key from trello.com/app-key |
| Token | `token` | password | Yes | 64-character OAuth token from trello.com/app-key |

### How it works

Every HTTP request appends:

```
?key={api_key}&token={token}
```

The `TrelloHTTPClient._auth_params()` method returns this dict, which is merged into every request via `_merge_params()`. **No Authorization header is set.**

### Install validation

During `install()`, the connector calls `GET /members/me`. A `401` or `403` response sets `auth_status=INVALID_CREDENTIALS` and `health=OFFLINE`. Any network failure sets `health=OFFLINE` with `auth_status=FAILED`.

### Security

- Credentials are stored encrypted in the Shielva vault and never logged.
- Tokens can be revoked at any time from [https://trello.com/your-profile/account](https://trello.com/your-profile/account) under **Applications**.

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
The Trello connector requires two install fields.

```json
{
  "api_key": "your_32_char_api_key",
  "token": "your_64_char_oauth_token"
}
```

### Internal defaults

| Parameter | Default | Description |
|-----------|---------|-------------|
| `TIMEOUT` | `30s` | HTTP request timeout |
| `MAX_ATTEMPTS` | `3` | Retry attempts on transient failures |
| `RETRY_BACKOFF` | Exponential | 1s â†’ 2s â†’ 4s with Â±0.5s jitter |
| `board filter` | `open` | Syncs open boards only |
| `card filter` | `open` | Syncs open cards only (via path: `/cards/open`) |

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: The connector exposes 8 API methods across 5 resource domains.

children.title: get_member(member_id='me')
children.content:
Fetch a Trello member by ID. Defaults to the authenticated user.

**Endpoint:** `GET /members/{member_id}?fields=id,username,fullName,email&key=...&token=...`

**Returns:** `dict` with keys `id`, `username`, `fullName`, `email`.

**Used by:** `install()`, `health_check()`.

children.title: list_boards(filter='open')
children.content:
List all boards for the authenticated member.

**Endpoint:** `GET /members/me/boards?filter={filter}&fields=id,name,desc,closed,dateLastActivity,prefs&key=...&token=...`

**Returns:** `list[dict]`. Each dict has `id`, `name`, `desc`, `closed`, `dateLastActivity`, `prefs`.

Note: Trello returns all boards in a single response â€” no pagination.

children.title: get_board(board_id, fields='id,name,desc,closed')
children.content:
Fetch a single Trello board by ID.

**Endpoint:** `GET /boards/{board_id}?fields={fields}&key=...&token=...`

**Returns:** `dict` with the requested fields.

children.title: list_board_lists(board_id, filter='open')
children.content:
Fetch all lists (columns) on a board.

**Endpoint:** `GET /boards/{board_id}/lists?filter={filter}&fields=id,name,closed,pos&key=...&token=...`

**Returns:** `list[dict]`. Each dict has `id`, `name`, `closed`, `pos`.

children.title: list_board_cards(board_id, filter='open')
children.content:
Fetch all cards on a board.

**Endpoint:** `GET /boards/{board_id}/cards/{filter}?fields=id,name,idList,desc,due,labels,members&key=...&token=...`

**Returns:** `list[dict]`. Each dict has `id`, `name`, `idList`, `desc`, `due`, `labels`, `members`.

Note: `filter` is a path segment (e.g., `/cards/open`, `/cards/all`), not a query parameter.

children.title: get_card(card_id)
children.content:
Fetch a single Trello card by ID with all fields.

**Endpoint:** `GET /cards/{card_id}?fields=all&key=...&token=...`

**Returns:** `dict` with all card fields.

children.title: list_board_members(board_id)
children.content:
Fetch all members of a board.

**Endpoint:** `GET /boards/{board_id}/members?fields=id,username,fullName&key=...&token=...`

**Returns:** `list[dict]`. Each dict has `id`, `username`, `fullName`.

children.title: list_board_labels(board_id)
children.content:
Fetch all labels defined on a board.

**Endpoint:** `GET /boards/{board_id}/labels&key=...&token=...`

**Returns:** `list[dict]`. Each dict has `id`, `name`, `color`, `idBoard`.

sections.title: Document Normalizers
sections.content:
The connector produces two document types.

### normalize_card(card, board_id)

Converts a raw Trello card into a `ConnectorDocument`:
- `id = sha256('card:' + card['id'])[:16]`
- `source = 'trello'`
- `type = 'card'`
- `title = card['name']`
- `content` â€” multiline string with name, description, list ID, board ID, due date, closed status, labels, members, and URL
- `source_url = card['url']`
- `metadata` â€” `card_id`, `board_id`, `list_id`, `due`, `closed`, `labels`, `member_names`

### normalize_board(board)

Converts a raw Trello board into a `ConnectorDocument`:
- `id = sha256('board:' + board['id'])[:16]`
- `source = 'trello'`
- `type = 'board'`
- `title = board['name']`
- `content` â€” multiline string with name, description, closed status, last activity date, and URL
- `source_url = 'https://trello.com/b/{board_id}'`
- `metadata` â€” `board_id`, `closed`, `date_last_activity`

sections.title: Sync Behaviour
sections.content:
The `sync(**kwargs)` method:

1. Fetches all open boards via `GET /members/me/boards?filter=open`
2. For each board:
   a. Normalizes the board into a `ConnectorDocument` (`type='board'`)
   b. Fetches all open cards via `GET /boards/{id}/cards/open`
   c. Normalizes each card into a `ConnectorDocument` (`type='card'`)
   d. If `kb_id` is provided via kwargs, ingests each document via `_ingest_document()`
3. Returns a `SyncResult` with `documents_found`, `documents_synced`, `documents_failed`

`documents_found` counts boards + cards. `SyncStatus.COMPLETED` if zero failures; `SyncStatus.PARTIAL` if any normalization fails; `SyncStatus.FAILED` if the boards or cards API call fails entirely.

**kwargs supported:**
- `kb_id` (str) â€” knowledge base ID to ingest into; if empty, documents are normalized but not ingested

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
### Exception hierarchy

```
TrelloError (base)            â€” all connector errors; has .message, .status_code, .code
â”śâ”€â”€ TrelloAuthError           â€” 401 / 403: invalid or revoked credentials
â”śâ”€â”€ TrelloNetworkError        â€” timeouts, connection failures, 5xx server errors
â”śâ”€â”€ TrelloNotFoundError       â€” 404: resource does not exist
â””â”€â”€ TrelloRateLimitError      â€” 429: too many requests; has .retry_after (seconds)
```

### Status code mapping (_raise_for_status)

| HTTP status | Exception |
|-------------|----------|
| 401, 403 | `TrelloAuthError` |
| 404 | `TrelloNotFoundError` |
| 429 | `TrelloRateLimitError` (retry_after from Retry-After header) |
| 5xx | `TrelloNetworkError` |
| other 4xx | `TrelloError` |

### Retry policy (with_retry)

- Max 3 attempts with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s) plus Â±0.5s jitter
- `TrelloAuthError` â€” never retried (requires human intervention)
- `TrelloRateLimitError` â€” honours `Retry-After` header when present
- All other `TrelloError` subclasses â€” retried up to 3 times

sections.title: Rate Limits
sections.content: Trello enforces a rate limit of **300 requests per 10 seconds** per token. The connector retries automatically on 429 responses. For large workspaces (many boards with many cards), consider spacing out sync jobs to avoid sustained rate-limit pressure.
